concerns.text: T069a requires sourcing ≥100 summaries with 'at least 20 per domain' across five domains, but there is no verification task to confirm the final `real_world_validation_labels.csv` actually achieves this stratified distribution per FR-031b. This is a critical coverage gap.
FR-012 requires handling missing baseline conversion rate, but no task explicitly verifies that entries with missing baseline are correctly flagged in audit notes as required.

T096 writes checksums to `data/checksums.txt` while T076 writes checksums to `output/checksums.txt`, creating two separate checksum files that violate Constitution Principle IV (Single Source of Truth).

FR-030 requires synthetic validation dataset with both binary AND continuous outcomes, but no task explicitly verifies that both outcome types are present in the generated synthetic data beyond T026's basic file creation check.
T020c archives provenance metadata, but there is no verification task confirming that all required metadata fields (URL, repository identifier, fetch timestamp) are captured for every summary.
T005 verifies CI compliance but doesn't confirm all Constitution Principles are actually implemented correctly in code. A dedicated task is needed to verify full Constitution compliance.
T083 verifies Quickstart execution time but doesn't explicitly verify execution on the 'default GitHub Actions runner' as specified in FR-028.
T026 mentions domain and year in task description, but no task explicitly verifies year extraction from source summaries is implemented.
